meaning: adv.令人沮丧地,令人惊愕地
examples: The final scene was dismayingly lacking in theatrical effect.
Six-character numeric passwords are dismayingly common, and the examples only go downhill from there.
Here, in this Ottawa winter, it is dismayingly easy to recall claims that Canada's capital city is the coldest after Ulan Bator.
Western donors made up much of the aid gap (Mongolians get among the highest number of aid dollars per head in the world), but to dismayingly small effect.
It's become a dismayingly rare pleasure to see a serious play on Broadway, and while this revival, like "Orphans" itself, is far from perfect, it's still very much worth seeing.
